authoritative_sources.title: Design and Construction Delivery Process
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA is an official federal public owner. Its delivery-process guidance is authoritative for the sequence of site acquisition, feasibility, architect-engineer work, construction management, and project management records used in architecture project execution.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the pack to require site, owner, and project-definition records before treating architecture work as ready to advance.

authoritative_sources.title: Defining the Architect's Basic Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AIA is the primary U.S. professional body for architects. Its basic-services guidance is a recognized architecture workflow baseline for schematic design, design development, construction documents, procurement support, and construction phase duties.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Phase progression and architect-owned workflow framing
used_for: boundary between project workflow support and architect services
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from collapsing into generic project coordination by grounding it in architecture-phase records and outputs.

authoritative_sources.title: Construction & Evaluation
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Council of Architectural Registration Boards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCARB is the U.S. licensure coordination body for architects. Its construction and evaluation guidance is authoritative for the boundary between construction-administration support and licensed architect judgment.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Construction administration and field exception routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Stops the lane from answering code, structural, or architect-of-record questions as if they were ordinary execution tasks.

authoritative_sources.title: Architect/Engineer Information and A/E Design Submission Requirements (PG-18-15)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs via Whole Building Design Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: VA is an official public owner and PG-18-15 is an official A/E submission manual defining required site, schematic, design-development, construction-document, and project-book deliverables for architecture work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Design submission assembly and review gating
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Anchors the submission-package model so phase advancement depends on actual drawings, specifications, studies, and project-book evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: A/E Cost Estimating Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs via Whole Building Design Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: VA's cost estimating manual is an official public-owner reference for estimate classifications, design-phase estimate maturity, and estimate deliverables that govern architecture project stage progression.
used_for: rework triggers when estimate support is absent
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes estimate sufficiency a real phase-gate dependency instead of an optional project-management note.

authoritative_sources.title: Project Requirements: Project Sign-Off and Completion
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NYC DOB is an authority having jurisdiction. Its project sign-off guidance defines inspection, objection-resolution, as-built, and final sign-off gates that constrain architecture project completion.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces the lane to treat sign-off and completion as jurisdiction-sensitive closures rather than generic task completion.

authoritative_sources.title: Facilities Standards for the Public Buildings Service (P100)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA P100 is an official federal design standard that establishes mandatory performance criteria, submission expectations, and review controls for public-building architecture projects.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Design standards control and document quality baseline
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds a standards-driven control layer so design packages are assessed against formal design criteria rather than schedule pressure alone.

authoritative_sources.title: Project Management Information System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's PMIS guidance is an official public-owner reference for the authoritative project record, milestone tracking, budget status, and document access controls used during architecture project execution.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to named project systems and retrieval hooks before it advances, holds, or closes architecture work.

authoritative_sources.title: COBie
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIBS publishes recognized building-information standards. COBie is the public handover-data standard for facility information exchanges that materially affects architecture closeout and turnover evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Turnover and owner handoff evidence capture
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from marking turnover complete when the information handoff package is incomplete or not tied to the authoritative project record.

workflow_stages.name: project grounding and site-readiness validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm project identity, jurisdiction, delivery method, site basis, owner program, existing conditions, and the governing systems of record before phase work advances.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: design submission and estimate progression
workflow_stages.description: Validate that the current SD, DD, or CD package, associated estimate maturity, consultant coordination, and approval records support movement to the next phase gate.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: permit and authority coordination
workflow_stages.description: Track permit submissions, AHJ comments, design responses, and approval routing without overstating architect-of-record, engineer-of-record, or permit-authority ownership.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: construction administration and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Handle RFI, submittal, ASI, and field-observation workflow states with explicit routing when code, structural, life-safety, or other licensed judgment is required.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: closeout and owner turnover
workflow_stages.description: Require punch, record drawings, O&M, training, commissioning, and turnover-information evidence before recommending substantial completion, sign-off support, or handoff closure.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: project charter, owner program, site survey, utility survey, geotechnical report, and basis-of-design records
schematic design, design-development, and construction-document submissions with drawing and specification issue history
architect-engineer estimate packages, value-engineering logs, scope changes, alternates, and approval records
permit applications, AHJ comments, response logs, registered-design-professional notes, and sign-off checklists
BIM or CAD model files, document-control transmittals, PMIS milestones, and approval registers
submittal logs, RFI logs, ASIs, meeting minutes, field reports, and nonconformance or punch records
record drawings, as-builts, O&M manuals, training signoffs, commissioning items, warranties, and COBie or equivalent handoff data
systems_of_record: project management information system or equivalent project controls platform
common data environment or document-control repository
permit portal and AHJ correspondence repository
construction administration log for RFIs, submittals, ASIs, and field observations
tenant authority matrix and policy library

allowed: Classify architecture project work as accepted, ready, in-progress, blocked, rework, or completed based on named site, submission, estimate, permit, construction-administration, and closeout evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed execution summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packets without claiming licensed design, code, permit, or financial approval authority.
Require the minimum tenant facts, source records, and system retrievals needed to continue safely.
escalate: Missing or conflicting site basis, estimate support, consultant comments, or approval records would change whether the project can advance.
Permit instructions, AHJ objections, contract terms, or tenant policy materially change the safe next action.
An RFI, submittal, field condition, or closeout package requires architect-of-record, licensed engineering, code, or commissioning authority.
refuse: Stamping or sealing, architect-of-record or engineer-of-record signoff, permit approval, or legal code interpretation.
Budget approval, change-order money approval, contract-claim resolution, or executive policy override.
Treating the industry name as cosmetic and answering without design submissions, estimate status, approvals, permit records, or turnover evidence.

domain_failure_modes: The lane acts like a generic project coordinator and ignores architecture-phase submissions, estimate maturity, permit records, and construction-administration artifacts.
Work is advanced even though site basis, owner program, consultant coordination, or estimate support is incomplete for the current phase gate.
Completion is claimed even though AHJ sign-off, punch closure, record drawings, O&M, training, or turnover data is still incomplete.
Submittal or RFI handling drifts into architect-of-record, licensed engineering, code, or safety-critical judgment.
Conflicting PMIS, CDE, permit, and closeout records are resolved informally instead of escalating the contradiction.
Renovation, new-build, and tenant-improvement workflows are flattened into one generic queue even though their authority boundaries and evidence differ materially.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific permit filing and sign-off obligations vary materially across AHJs and may shift between owner, expeditor, and registered design professional.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require the governing jurisdiction, permit matrix, and tenant authority map before applying permit-progression or completion decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Delivery method and contract form materially change who controls submittal review, changes, and closeout acceptance.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant retrieval of the contract form, review workflow, and approval matrix before applying construction-administration or turnover decisions.
